title: How Trump and His Advisers Miscalculated Iran’s Response to War
summary: In the lead-up to the U.S.-Israeli attack, President Trump downplayed the risks to the energy markets as a short-term concern that should not overshadow the mission to decapitate the Iranian regime.

title: How Trump’s War With Iran Changed the World in a Week
summary: The conflict is reshaping travel patterns, energy dependencies, living costs, trade routes and diplomatic alliances.

title: U.S. Gas Prices Jump for 11th Straight Day, and Oil Pushes Higher
summary: Drivers are paying an average of 20 percent more at the pump since the strikes on Iran began. The global oil price was up and stocks in Asia rose.

title: Iranians Feel the War Is ‘Closing In,’ and Bondi Is Said to Move Because of Threats
summary: Plus, iPads in kindergarten.

title: Trump Casts a Shadow Over One of Mexico’s Deadliest States
summary: President Trump wants to strike cartels inside Mexico. In Sinaloa State, a cartel stronghold, some residents said they were willing to entertain U.S. intervention.

title: Bondi Is Said to Move to Military Housing Because of Threats
summary: The attorney general relocated from a Washington apartment to a base in the area within the past month, according to people familiar with the situation.

title: Trump Administration to Restart Global Entry Program
summary: The program for people traveling internationally, set to come back online at 5 a.m. Wednesday, had been paused amid the shutdown of the Homeland Security Department.

title: They Don’t Want Their Company’s Surveillance Tool Used by ICE
summary: Thomson Reuters, best known for its media outlet and legal research tools, provides an investigative tool to immigration enforcers. Its Minnesota employees want that to stop.

title: Why Did the UK Police Repeatedly Decline to Investigate Claims About Epstein and Prince Andrew?
summary: The police in London interviewed Virginia Giuffre three times over her allegations about Jeffrey Epstein, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and Ghislaine Maxwell, but never began a criminal investigation.

title: Clayton Fuller and Shawn Harris Advance to Runoff in Marjorie Taylor Greene’s Georgia District
summary: Clayton Fuller will face Shawn Harris, a Democrat, in an April runoff. Mr. Fuller’s win over a flashier Republican showed the power of the president’s support.

title: Bennie Thompson Defeats Young Challenger in Mississippi Primary
summary: The victory by Mr. Thompson, the state’s longest-serving Black Democrat, offers a counterpoint to the broader push nationwide for younger leadership in Congress.

title: Liza Minnelli: What, Specifically, Makes Her an Icon
summary: The things that make Liza Liza.

title: Book Review: ‘Kids, Wait Till You Hear This! A Memoir,’ by Liza Minnelli
summary: “Kids, Wait Till You Hear This!” is a familiar reminder that growing up in showbiz can lead to awards and adulation, but also to heartache.

title: Ukraine Can Now Manufacture ‘China-Free’ Drones
summary: The country has prioritized self-sufficiency in producing a crucial battlefield weapon, though weaning itself fully off cheaper Chinese components is difficult.

title: Russia Says Ukraine’s Strike on Factory City Was Deadly
summary: Ukraine said the attack struck a key electronics plant, highlighting how it remains capable of hitting high-value targets inside Russia.

title: Raise Taxes on the Rich? These Rich New Yorkers Are All for It.
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani says he wants to raise taxes on New Yorkers who earn more than $1 million per year. Some millionaires actually agree with him.

title: A Trump-Xi Summit Nears, but China Doesn’t Know What Trump Wants
summary: Trump’s agenda for the high-stakes meeting remains unclear to Beijing, Chinese analysts say, while American executives say they haven’t been invited along.

title: China’s Military Has Quietly Cut Flying Near Taiwan. But Why?
summary: For years, China has flown military jets near Taiwan almost daily. Then they suddenly stopped, leaving analysts to wonder why.

title: How ‘Sync Music’ Became the Soundtrack to Our Lives
summary: “Sync music” has become the soundtrack to our lives — whether we realize it or not.

title: In Talking to Parents About Vaccines, Pediatricians Navigate a Sea of Misinformation
summary: Practitioners nationwide are striving to do what’s best for children’s health, while staying supportive in the face of mistrust and confusion.

title: Slowly, Slowly, ‘Darwin’s Finches of the Snail World’ Return From Near Extinction
summary: Partula snails all but vanished from Polynesia after the arrival of a carnivorous foreign snail. But a global alliance of zoos has worked to bring them back.

title: Why Voters Might Finally Blame Trump for Rising Costs
summary: Trump’s Iran war will affect more than just gas prices.

summary: The Weather Service issued several of its most urgent alerts on Tuesday night.

title: A Dublin Pub-Crawl, but Hold the Booze
summary: A wander through some famed watering holes in Ireland’s capital reveals a booming alcohol-free scene and a cultural shift.

title: Alberto Carvalho, Suspended LAUSD Chief, Denies Wrongdoing
summary: Through his lawyers, Alberto Carvalho, who was put on leave after the F.B.I. raided his home and office, said that his actions were appropriate but that he would respect the investigative process.

title: Bumblebee Queens Can Breathe Underwater
summary: A new study offers clues as to how the insects survive flooding as they emerge from a hibernation-like phase every winter.
